title: INtopBackup
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Trump fires Kristi Noem as DHS chief, names Sen. Markwayne Mullin to replace her
items.description: President Trump has fired his homeland security secretary, Kristi Noem, and said Markwayne Mullin, a senator from Oklahoma, would replace her.

items.title: Can a digital tablet cut back a country's overuse of antibiotics?
items.description: Overprescribing antibiotics breeds antibiotic resistance. A new tool aims to lower a notably high rate of such prescriptions in Rwanda.

items.title: They were led off course in a big race. But a fix is more complicated than prize money

items.title: A Jan. 6 rioter pardoned by Trump was sentenced to life in prison for child sex abuse
items.description: Since receiving presidential pardons, dozens of former Capitol rioters have gotten into more legal trouble. In Florida, Andrew Paul Johnson was senten...

items.title: Travel industry pushes Congress to end DHS shutdown and pay federal security workers
items.description: With the busy spring break travel season looming, travel and aviation industry leaders urged Congress to end the stalemate over DHS funding before wor...

items.title: U.S. contractor who allegedly stole $46 million of crypto arrested, FBI says
items.description: John Daghita was arrested on the island of Saint Martin, FBI Director Kash Patel said.

items.title: Videos from officers show terrifying moments during Texas mass shooting that left 3 dead

'We have been preparing': Why the boots on the ground in Iran could be Kurdish
items.description: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups in exile in northern Iraq tell the BBC they have plans to cross the border but deny already doing so.

items.title: Record share of Americans are tapping 401(k)s for hardship withdrawals
items.description: More Americans are digging into their retirement savings for emergency expenses, research from Vanguard shows.

items.title: Watch Live: Pete Hegseth, Adm. Brad Cooper give news conference
items.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will give a press briefing with Admiral Brad Cooper, the commander of U.S. Central Command.

items.title: Israelis back war with Iran despite uncertainty and fatigue
items.description: Israelis broadly support the US-Israeli military campaign with Iran, even if they are tired of air raid alerts and constant upheaval.

items.title: Iran's high-risk war strategy seems to centre on endurance and deterrence
items.description: Tehran's approach appears to rest on a belief it can absorb strikes longer than its adversaries sustain pain and costs, writes BBC Persian's Amir Azim...

items.title: Israeli evacuation order for Beirut's southern suburbs sparks panic
items.description: Huge traffic jams form after the unprecedented warning for people to leave the Hezbollah stronghold, as Israel steps up its strikes across Lebanon.

items.title: Checkpoints everywhere and internet blackouts: Life in Iran as US-Israeli strikes intensify
items.description: Iranians are coping with daily strikes and security crackdowns - all while trying to stay in touch with loved ones.

items.title: Trump says he must have a role in choosing Iran's next leader as war expands
items.description: President Trump said he must have a role in choosing Iran's next leader and called the son of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei "unacceptable."
